skill.name: performance-optimization
skill.description: Guides full-stack performance analysis with templates for databases, APIs, caching, frontend rendering, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Diagnose bottlenecks and apply practical optimization patterns across the application stack.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many suspicious patterns, but review shows they are markdown examples, local diagnostic commands, React code-splitting imports, and performance SQL snippets. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found. The remaining risk is low because users could copy example commands or MD5 ETag code into inappropriate contexts.

low_findings.title: Documentation Commands Require User Approval Before Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged external command findings in SKILL.md are example Lighthouse and bundle analysis commands inside a markdown code block. They are not executed by the skill, but an agent should only run them with user approval and in the intended project context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visibly fenced documentation examples and there is no script wrapper or automation path. The low residual risk is that users or agents may copy and run npx-based commands.

low_findings.title: MD5 Hash Example Is Non-Security ETag Code
low_findings.description: The API template uses MD5 to generate ETags for cache validation. This is not password hashing, signing, or authentication, but it should not be copied for security-sensitive integrity checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code context names the function generateETag and uses the digest for HTTP conditional requests. MD5 is still a weak algorithm, so misuse outside ETags remains a realistic low-risk concern.

low_findings.title: Performance Reconnaissance Patterns Are Benign Diagnostics
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SQL and analysis patterns as reconnaissance. The reviewed snippets are PostgreSQL performance diagnostics and local Lighthouse checks intended for systems the user controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SQL examples inspect query plans and slow-query statistics for optimization. No network scanning, credential harvesting, or unauthorized target discovery appears in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Standard Frontend Code Splitting
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings are React lazy-loading examples for local route components. They do not load remote code or construct import paths from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both import paths are static local strings inside React lazy calls. This strongly supports a code-splitting use case rather than a script loading threat.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Hash Algorithm In Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: MD5 appears in an ETag generation example. It is acceptable for non-security cache validators, but dangerous if reused for passwords, signatures, or tamper protection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly calls createHash with md5. The surrounding ETag context lowers severity but does not remove the risk of unsafe reuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example CLI Commands In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents Lighthouse and bundle analyzer commands. These are ordinary performance tools, but they should not run automatically or against untrusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present as markdown examples and target localhost or package tooling. There is no evidence that the skill executes them automatically.

content.user_title: Optimize Application Performance Across the Stack
content.value_statement: Slow applications create poor user experiences and raise infrastructure costs. This skill helps teams find bottlenecks and apply proven optimizations for databases, APIs, caching, frontend rendering, and monitoring.
actual_capabilities: Map bottlenecks across frontend, API, database, cache, and monitoring layers.
Provide database optimization patterns for N+1 queries, indexes, connection pools, and pagination.
Suggest API improvements for compression, ETags, field selection, and cursor pagination.
Guide frontend improvements using React memoization, virtualization, lazy loading, and smaller imports.
Define performance budgets and metrics for Core Web Vitals, API latency, and cache hit rates.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not automatic production changes.
Templates need adaptation to the target framework, data model, and deployment environment.
It does not replace profiling with real production or staging measurements.
It does not verify cloud, CDN, or database settings without user-provided details.

use_cases.title: Reduce API and database latency
use_cases.description: Find slow queries, remove N+1 patterns, add pagination, and improve response caching.

use_cases.title: Improve page speed and rendering
use_cases.description: Apply code splitting, memoization, virtualization, image strategy, and Core Web Vitals targets.

use_cases.title: Create a performance improvement plan
use_cases.description: Set budgets, define monitoring metrics, prioritize bottlenecks, and track improvements over time.

prompt_templates.title: Find The Slow Layer
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for an unknown performance problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this performance symptom and available metrics. Identify the most likely slow layer, explain what to measure next, and suggest the first three safe checks.

prompt_templates.title: Tune A Slow Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this endpoint, database access pattern, and response shape. Recommend changes for query count, indexes, caching, field selection, compression, and pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Frontend Responsiveness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced frontend rendering and bundle review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React page for rendering cost, bundle size, layout shifts, and interaction delay. Propose specific changes and expected measurement impact.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Performance Program
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a production application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a performance program for this application. Include budgets, monitoring metrics, alert thresholds, profiling workflow, rollout order, and regression prevention.

output_examples.input: A user dashboard takes four seconds to load after login.
output: Likely bottlenecks: database joins, API payload size, and dashboard bundle weight.
Measure server timing, query duration, cache hit rate, and largest contentful paint.
Start with query profiling, response field selection, and route-level code splitting.

output_examples.input: A product list page becomes slow with large catalogs.
output: Replace full-list loading with cursor pagination and stable sort order.
Add indexes for the filter and sort fields used by the page.
Use list virtualization and cache common filter results with clear invalidation rules.

output_examples.input: An engineering manager needs performance targets for a release.
output: Set measurable budgets for API latency, bundle size, image weight, and Core Web Vitals.
Add alerts for regression trends, not only hard outages.
Review results weekly and connect each optimization to a measured bottleneck.

best_practices: Profile first, then optimize the measured bottleneck with the highest user impact.
Set performance budgets before release and enforce them in continuous integration.
Pair each cache with a clear invalidation rule and an observable hit-rate metric.
anti_patterns: Optimizing code paths without confirming they affect user-visible performance.
Loading every record or relation when pagination and field selection would be enough.
Adding caches without ownership, expiry, invalidation, or monitoring.

faq.question: What does this skill help optimize?
faq.answer: It helps with databases, APIs, caching, frontend rendering, bundle size, Core Web Vitals, and monitoring.

faq.question: Can it change my application automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis patterns and templates that a developer must adapt and apply.

faq.question: Does it require production metrics?
faq.answer: Production or staging metrics improve accuracy, but the skill can start from symptoms, traces, logs, or code snippets.

faq.question: Is the MD5 example safe?
faq.answer: It is shown for non-security ETags only. Do not use MD5 for passwords, signatures, or security checks.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How should teams use the templates?
faq.answer: Treat them as starting points. Validate each change with profiling, tests, and environment-specific review.
